_readme: Ground truth for every video Curio has actually POSTED — creative metadata + platform-separated analytics, matched to Instagram insights screenshots on 2026-07-12. This file is the durable machine copy; docs/curio/EXPERIMENT_LEDGER.md is the human trail. Import with: npx tsx tools/import_posted_experiments.ts (content-addressed rows; corrections here supersede stale rows via latest-wins). RULES: combined IG+FB view totals are NOT an optimization target; a metrics row is only imported when viewsInstagram is known. Every experiment carries a `provenance` block separating observed / derived / estimated / user-reported values — treat estimated values as approximate.
collectedAtNote: original four-post batch collected 2026-07-12; Dead Water (posted-deadwater) screenshots added 2026-07-16
source: Instagram Reel insights screenshots (samples/Curio Video Stats/* collected 2026-07-12; Dead Water reel screenshots 2026-07-16) + ffprobe/ebur128 measurements of the posted / closest-match local files + reviewer screenshot readings (Codex, 2026-07-12 and 2026-07-16)

experiments.title: Third Man Factor
experiments.postedAtProvenance: inferred — views graph starts Jun 30; exact post time unverified

creative.archetype: atmospheric survival mystery with immediate visual evidence
creative.openingFrame: footprints visible at t=0 — the anomaly before the explanation
creative.hookWording: When survivors are about to give up… someone appears right beside them
creative.visualTimeline: footprints → mountains → sailors → polar environments → distant person → struggling survivor → named phenomenon
creative.reveal: 'The Third Man Factor' named at ~70-80% of runtime; supernatural reading NOT fully eliminated (residual mystery)

analytics.splitNote: IG/FB split from Leon's 2026-07-12 22:49 Reel-insights screenshots: 240 IG + 366 FB = 606. FB supplied 60% of views — consistent with the other three posts. The importer writes the IG-only stream.
analytics.retentionAtEndProvenance: estimated from the retention graph shape — approximate, not a platform number
analytics.reachNote: 165 'Accounts reached' sits on the combined summary card (views card on the same screen shows the 606 total), so the reach surface is AMBIGUOUS — could be IG-only or IG+FB. Kept as reachInstagram per the earlier record, flagged unverified.
analytics.engagementCountsNote: likes/comments/shares/saves/reposts are the counts shown on the IG reel header; whether FB crosspost interactions are included is unverified. Platform-displayed like rate 8.0% does not equal 16/240 (6.7%) or 16/606 (2.6%) — Meta's rate denominator is undocumented; raw counts are the source of truth here.

userReported: skipRate, rates, profileSourceShare, visual timeline and hook wording (Leon's 2026-07-12 analysis of the video + insights)
unverified: reachInstagram (165 shown on combined summary card — surface ambiguous)

experiments.verdict: ONLY clear retention winner (skip 20.5-34.8pp better than every other post; only video with nonzero shares/saves/reposts/comments)

experiments.title: 300 Years Boat (Fata Morgana) v1
experiments.postedAtProvenance: inferred from the export filename date — unverified

creative.openingFrame: dark storm-ship composition, premise not yet stated
creative.hookWording: For 300 years, sailors who saw this thought… (sentence FRAGMENT — premise arrives late)
creative.visualTimeline: single ship composition held through most of the hook; floating ship/fog/false horizon later
creative.reveal: optical illusion (false horizon) — the fascinating idea is delayed

analytics.retentionAtEndProvenance: estimated from the retention graph shape — approximate

derived: like/share/save counts in the import (IG views x rate, rounded)
userReported: skipRate, rates, hook wording, timeline (Leon's analysis)

experiments.verdict: aesthetic liked (6.3% like rate, 2nd best) but comprehension too slow: ~half the audience gone in the first seconds

experiments.title: Can't Forget Anything (hyperthymesia)
experiments.postedAtProvenance: inferred from the export filename date — unverified

creative.archetype: what-if explainer with generic symbolic imagery
creative.openingFrame: glowing brain silhouette — generic stock/AI-health look; no person, conflict, evidence, or mystery
creative.hookWording: What if you could never forget anything? (reads as a superpower; the painful contradiction lands late)
creative.visualTimeline: multiple distinct scenes (full shot list not yet audited); OPENING scene is the verified generic brain silhouette — the retention leak is the opening, not necessarily the whole edit

analytics.retentionAtEndProvenance: estimated from the retention graph shape — approximate

derived: engagement counts in the import (IG views x rate, rounded)
userReported: skipRate, rates, exploreSourceShare, opening-frame description (Leon's analysis)

experiments.verdict: topic has save/share pull (1.6% each + 23.6% Explore sampling) but the generic opening lost the sampled audience — REWRITE AND RETEST the concept, don't discard

experiments.title: 300 Years Boat Retry
experiments.sourceFile: samples/Curio Video Stats/300 Years Boat Retry/My Video-5.MP4

creative.archetype: atmospheric mystery, longer/more-explicit revision (UNCONTROLLED retry: hook, duration, pacing, first shot AND editing all changed at once)
creative.hookWording: more explicit opening, small serif phrases revealed gradually
creative.premiseNote: the basic upside-down-ship premise reads within ~2-3s (reviewer correction); the FAILURE was pacing after the premise — slower, less visually progressive first stretch — not premise opacity
creative.visualTimeline: longer hold on one composition; more atmospheric setup before explanation

analytics.retentionAtEndProvenance: estimated from the retention graph shape — approximate

derived: engagement counts in the import (IG views x rate, rounded)

experiments.verdict: revision made everything WORSE (skip 59.1%→73.4%, likes 6.3%→0.9%, all sharing to zero): pacing after the premise got slower and less visually progressive; also proof that changing five variables at once teaches nothing

experiments.title: Dead Water (In the Arctic)
experiments.postedAtProvenance: inferred from the views graph beginning Jul 16; exact posting time unverified
experiments.sourceFileNote: Closest LOCAL visual match is data/productions/REP-2/REP-2-captions-export-v3-leveled.mp4. v1/v2/v3 (each with a -leveled variant) share the same underlying master, duration (18.833s / 1080x1920@30), and imagery, but their CAPTION treatments (sizes / leveling) are NOT visually identical, so the exact posted export CANNOT be determined without upload evidence. Screenshot runtime (~18s) corroborates the REP-2 family (18.83s) but does not confirm any single file.

creative.archetype: atmospheric strange-science mystery; CONTEXT-FIRST opening (location/atmosphere before the anomaly)
creative.durationProvenance: observed on the local closest-match REP-2 exports (18.833s); screenshot showed ~18s. Posted-file duration unverified.
creative.resolutionProvenance: observed on local REP-2 exports; posted-file unverified
creative.openingFrame: atmospheric Arctic ship + 'IN THE ARCTIC' title card — geographic/atmospheric CONTEXT, not the anomaly
creative.hookWording: 'In the Arctic…' location/context framing; the impossible premise (a ship mysteriously halted — 'dead water') is NOT stated at frame zero
creative.premiseNote: the contradiction (what is impossible) is not presented at frame zero — the opening leads with location + a mostly-static beautiful ship. User verdict: context, not anomaly.
creative.visualTimeline: conservative reconstruction from the UNVERIFIED local REP-2 family (not confirmed as the posted file): 'IN THE ARCTIC' title over an atmospheric ship in ice -> held / slow ship-in-fog compositions -> the ship stalled and held motionless -> captioned explanation of the 'dead water' internal-wave phenomenon near the end. Exact shot list and timing unverified.
creative.visualTimelineProvenance: derived from the local closest-match REP-2 exports — UNVERIFIED as the posted file
creative.reveal: 'dead water' phenomenon (a ship slowed/held by sub-surface internal waves) — factual payoff; whether it carried social currency is unproven

audio.provenance: observed on the LOCAL closest-match REP-2-captions-export-v3-leveled.mp4 via ffmpeg ebur128 — UNVERIFIED as the posted file

analytics.splitNote: IG 224 (10.8%) + FB 1849 (89.2%) = 2073. Facebook supplied ~89.2% of plays. The combined total is NOT a creative win and NOT an optimization target; the IG stream is the creative signal and the importer writes IG-only. IG's 224 ≈ Third Man's 240 → NO Instagram reach breakout.
analytics.reachScopeNote: '162 accounts reached' — platform scope AMBIGUOUS (may be IG-only while 2073 is IG+FB). Do NOT compute views-per-account-reached from these screenshots. Recorded as reachAccounts (surface unverified), NOT as reachInstagram.
analytics.watchRatioProvenance: derived 8s / 18.83s (local family duration) = 0.425; plausible range 0.425-0.444 because Instagram displays only a rounded 0:18

retentionCurve.provenance: estimated from the retention graph shape — approximate, not platform numbers

analytics.engagementCountsNote: Raw counts on the IG reel header: 9 likes; 0 comments/shares/saves/reposts. Displayed rates: like 0.6%, share/save/comment/repost 0.0%. The PLATFORM SCOPE of these engagement counts/rates AND of the average-watch figure is NOT explicitly labeled (IG-only vs IG+FB) — treat the scope as unverified. Meta's rate denominator is undocumented; do NOT recompute the displayed 0.6% from 9/224 (which would give 4.0%). Raw counts are the source of truth. EVERY high-intent action (share/save/comment/repost/follow) is ZERO.
analytics.viewSourcesNote: 87.2% Reels-tab + 9.6% Explore = mostly COLD distribution → the opening-hook failure is a genuine audience verdict on NEW viewers, not an existing-follower artifact.

observed: skipRate (58.5% — directly shown on the supplied screenshot)
duration/resolution/audio of the LOCAL closest-match REP-2 file ONLY (not confirmed as the posted file)
derived: visualTimeline (reconstructed from the unverified local REP-2 family)
estimated: postedAt (inferred from the views-graph start; exact time unverified)
userReported: 'opening is context not anomaly' analysis, component verdicts, capturedAt (Leon/Codex 2026-07-16)
unverified: sourceFile — exact posted export (closest local match: REP-2-captions-export-v3-leveled.mp4)
platform scope of engagement counts/rates and of avgWatchTime — IG-only vs IG+FB not labeled

experiments.verdict: NOT a creative win despite 2,073 combined plays: FB supplied ~89%, IG only 224 (≈ Third Man's 240 → no IG reach breakout), and every high-intent action = 0. Retention pins the failure to the OPENING: ~45% gone by ~3s, then the curve stabilizes (~7-9s onward) → the subject was not the problem, the context-first scroll-stop was. Improved avg viewing DEPTH over the older boat posts but did NOT solve their shared opening problem. REJECTED (causal, repeated evidence): context-first opening, static beauty as scroll-stop, combined-total-as-success. OBSERVED FACT: every high-intent action (share/save/comment/repost/follow) was 0. PROVISIONAL inference (NOT causally proven — the opening failure also shrank the audience that reached the payoff): the payoff may not have produced enough repeatable/social value. NOT REJECTED: the Dead Water topic. vs Third Man (240 IG / 38.6% skip / ~59.5% watch / real shares+saves+comments) → Dead Water (224 IG / 58.5% skip / ~42.5% watch / zero high-intent).

experiments.title: Harrison Okene (The Hand Grabbed Back)
experiments.postedAtProvenance: inferred — final deliverable built the evening of Jul 17 and first insights screenshots landed Jul 18; exact post time unverified
experiments.liveStatus: LIVE and actively growing on Facebook as of 2026-07-19 — FROZEN: do not edit/delete/rebuild/repost/boost
experiments.sourceFileNote: Posted frame zero = the glitchy (Veo) hand grab — Leon-confirmed 2026-07-19 — which matches the v3 lineage (local closest match: data/productions/HARRISON-OKENE/preview-okene-v3.mp4, 270x480@30, 19.633s). Screenshot shows captions NOT present in that local file, so the posted file is a captioned derivative that does NOT exist on this machine (Downloads/iCloud/samples swept 2026-07-19). All local captioned exports are v5-based and RULED OUT by frame-zero mismatch, including ~/Downloads/a_hand_inside_the_wreck…_20260717_1717.mp4 (1080x1920@30, 19.633s, v5 imagery). Posted resolution, captions, and audio loudness are therefore UNVERIFIED; if captioned via the Captions.ai app without a re-mux, audio was likely renormalized to ~-13.3 LUFS.

creative.archetype: true survival mystery; ANOMALY-FIRST opening — an impossible visible human action on frame zero
creative.durationProvenance: observed on the local v3 lineage (19.633s, shared by every Okene export); IG displays a rounded 0:19. Posted-file duration itself unverified.
creative.resolution: 270x480@30 on the local v3 closest match — POSTED-FILE RESOLUTION UNVERIFIED
creative.openingFrame: a hand emerges from the dark wreck and grabs the diver back — physically impossible-looking human contact at t=0; visible AI defects in the generated hand (Veo lineage)
creative.hookWording: posted caption text UNVERIFIED; locked VO opens 'A hand inside the wreck grabbed the diver back'
creative.visualTimeline: hand-grab anomaly -> alive underwater -> 60 hours -> 100 feet down -> breathing trapped air -> engineered silence -> surfacing could kill him -> pressurized bell -> two more days decompressing (reconstructed from the v3-lineage production; posted export unverified)
creative.reveal: the rescue itself could have killed him — decompression as the second trap after the apparent rescue

audio.provenance: locked audio master QA values — POSTED-FILE audio unverified (Captions.ai app renormalizes to ~-13.3 LUFS if no re-mux was done)

creative.producedWith: factory v3 lineage (Veo hero motion) + unknown external captioning pass

analytics.splitNote: Checkpoint 2 (2026-07-19): IG 178 (0.7%) + FB 26,165 (99.3%) = 26,343. Combined total is NOT an optimization target; the importer writes the IG-only stream. FACEBOOK VIEW CAVEAT: Facebook counts a Reel view at 1 ms of playback, so the FB headline count is a distribution signal, not proof of meaningful watches.
analytics.reachScopeNote: '113 accounts reached' (2026-07-18 screenshot) — platform scope AMBIGUOUS and clearly NOT Facebook-inclusive (26k views over 113 accounts would be absurd). Recorded as reachAccounts (surface unverified), NOT reachInstagram.
analytics.avgWatchScopeNote: 13s average watch, 36.5% skip, retention curve and view sources all sit on an Instagram-oriented insights surface (several graphs explicitly labeled Instagram-only). They may describe IG only and MUST NOT be used to explain Facebook distribution. FB-native retention/watch-time NOT yet captured (Graph API token expired; renewal pending).

retentionCurve.provenance: estimated from the IG retention graph shape (2026-07-18 screenshot) — approximate, Instagram-scoped

analytics.retentionAtEndProvenance: estimated from graph shape — approximate, Instagram-scoped
analytics.engagementCountsNote: likes=20 is the 2026-07-18 IG reel-header reading (scope unverified, consistent with prior posts). The 153 'reactions' figure (2026-07-19) is scope-AMBIGUOUS — almost certainly FB-dominant given the view split — and is deliberately NOT mapped to the IG likes field or the learning stream. EVERY high-intent action (share/save/comment/repost/follow) remains ZERO at both checkpoints.
analytics.viewSourcesNote: Instagram-scoped top-sources card (2026-07-18): 85.5% Reels tab + 8.1% Explore = cold discovery test on IG; IG sampled it and stopped expanding.

checkpoints.note: checkpoint 1 — PRESERVED as time-series evidence, never overwrite
checkpoint 2 — FB grew ~6.5x in ~1 day while IG added +2; FB supplies 99.3% of all plays

analytics.facebookNativeInsights: NOT CAPTURED — Graph API token expired 2026-07-14 (re-tested 2026-07-19, OAuthException 190/463). Pending Leon's renewal (pages_show_list, pages_read_engagement, read_insights, Curio Page selected). Required once available: unique reach/viewers, 3-second views, avg watch time + retention, completion rate, recommendation-vs-follower traffic, FB-native shares/comments, Page follows — plus 24h/72h/7d snapshots.

observed: skipRate 36.5%, avgWatchTime 13s, viewSources — IG-oriented insights surface
duration/resolution/audio of LOCAL v3-lineage files only (posted file not obtained)
META token expiry (Graph API error re-tested 2026-07-19)
userReported: checkpoint-2 numbers (FB 26,165 / IG 178 / 153 reactions) and 'still growing' status (Leon/Codex 2026-07-19)
posted Reel starts with the glitchy hand (Leon 2026-07-19) — basis for the v3-lineage identification
unverified: sourceFile — exact posted captioned export (v3-lineage confirmed by frame zero, file NOT on this machine)
posted resolution / caption text / audio loudness
platform scope of reachAccounts (113), avgWatchTime, skipRate, retention, engagement counts, and the 153 reactions

experiments.verdict: FACEBOOK DISTRIBUTION WINNER, NOT YET AN ENGAGEMENT OR CONVERSION WINNER. Three gates: [1] Scroll-stop PASS — best IG skip yet (36.5%) AND Facebook keeps widening recommendation (3,998 -> 26,165 in ~a day). [2] Retention PASS/partial on the IG-scoped surface only (~66% avg watch, ~33% end); FB-native retention unknown and the 1ms FB view definition inflates the headline. [3] Advocacy FAIL — zero shares/saves/comments/reposts/follows at 26k+ combined plays; 153 reactions is passive approval. IG plateaued at 178 while FB ran the distribution test — do NOT explain the FB growth with IG-scoped metrics. Frame-zero impossible human evidence + escalating single-story danger is the best current Curio opening pattern; the Veo hand defects and the procedural (non-transferable) ending are NOT to be repeated.
